Extensions.File:
Claude finished the response create a catchy word doc that says ATTENTION!
As of today MDT has been disabled. For OSD deployments we will now be using OSDCloud.
Press F12 to boot into network boot and select OSDCloud - Production. Once selected you will receive a powershell prompt to select the CompanyID of the device you are imaging. If there is no CompanyID, then type Z and hit Enter. Press enter again confirming that the fixed disks on the device will be erased.
After you start the process OSDCloud will download the latest Windows 11 25H2 installer, identify the device you are on and download/install the Drivers for your device as well as apply the latest patches.
Once the deployment process is completed the device will reboot and login to Windows. After the windows log in completes OSDCloud will wrap up by renaming the computer and deploying the RMM agent. Your device name will rename itself upon reboot.
RMM should create a local admin account depending on the CompanyID selected. If there are issues and a local admin account is not created you can temporarily use the Built-In Administrator account using the credentials below.
Username - Administrator Password - OSDCloudD3pl0y1!
YOU NEED TO CREATE A LOCAL ADMIN ACCOUNT AND DISABLE THE BUILT IN ADMIN ACCOUNT BEFORE THE DEVICE GOES INTO PRODUCTION/USE.
Show more
Assessed document formatting capability for technical content Assessed document formatting capability for technical content That number badges are stretched a bit oddly but overall it looks clean — let me check page 2 before finalizing.
Check rendered page 2 Check rendered page 2 Good, it splits cleanly (the pink credentials box just spills onto page 2, which is fine). Copying to outputs now.
Ran a command, read a file Ran a command, read a file Here's the announcement doc — big red ATTENTION banner up top, numbered step blocks walking through the OSDCloud deployment process, a highlighted box for the fallback Administrator credentials, and a bold black "required before production use" warning at the bottom.
Osdcloud deployment announcement Document · DOCX Can you fit this on a single page?
Strategized layout compression through spacing and font adjustments Strategized layout compression through spacing and font adjustments Now let me tighten spacing throughout — banner, headers, step blocks, and font sizes.
Ran 2 commands Ran 2 commands Now let's tighten the step blocks (title/body font sizes and spacing) and bodyPara/codeRun sizes.
Tighten step block fonts and spacing Tighten step block fonts and spacing Now the bullet list items, note paragraph, and the local admin credentials box / final warning box.
Ran 2 commands Ran 2 commands Now the final warning banner and the spacer paragraph before it.
Tighten final warning banner Tighten final warning banner Also tighten the "RMM should automatically..." bodyPara before the credentials box, and step body texts sizes for step 2/3.
Ran 2 commands Ran 2 commands Single page now. Let's view it to check the layout looks good.
Check the single-page rendered document Check the single-page rendered document It fits neatly on a single page now, with everything still readable. Copying it to outputs.
Ran a command, read a file Ran a command, read a file Fits on one page now, with plenty of room to spare.
Osdcloud deployment announcement Document · DOCX When i run the script it closes the prompt screen and leaves the powershell windows open. I want it to show the prompt screen and close powershell until Ok it clicked
<# .SYNOPSIS Detects Desktop vs Laptop via WMI/CIM, then renames the computer: MGE-DeviceType-Last7OfSerial (e.g., MGE-LT-1234567) Runs fully unattended — no prompts, no confirmations, no auto-restart.
.NOTES Run as Administrator. Requires a restart (handled separately) to apply the new computer name. Designed for RMM/ZTI deployment (NinjaOne, N-central, OSDCloud, etc.) #>
#region --- Config --- $CompanyID = "KIME" #endregion
#region --- Get Serial Number FIRST (bail out early if missing) --- try { $serial = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_BIOS -ErrorAction Stop).SerialNumber } catch { throw "No serial number found. Unable to query Win32_BIOS: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($serial) -or $serial -match '^(To Be Filled By O.E.M.|None|System Serial Number|0+)$') { throw "No serial number found. Computer name was NOT changed." }
$serialSuffix = if ($serial.Length -lt 7) { $serial } else { $serial.Substring($serial.Length - 7) } #endregion
#region --- Detect Desktop vs Laptop --- function Get-DeviceType {
$pcSystemType = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_ComputerSystem -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).PCSystemType $chassisTypes = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_SystemEnclosure -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).ChassisTypes $hasBattery = [bool](Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Battery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
$laptopChassis = @(8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 18, 21, 30, 31, 32)
$votes = @()
if ($pcSystemType -eq 2) { $votes += "LT" } elseif ($pcSystemType -eq 1) { $votes += "DT" }
if ($chassisTypes) { $match = $chassisTypes | Where-Object { $laptopChassis -contains $_ } if ($match) { $votes += "LT" } else { $votes += "DT" } }
if ($hasBattery) { $votes += "LT" }
if ($votes.Count -eq 0) { return $null }
$ltCount = ($votes | Where-Object { $_ -eq "LT" }).Count $dtCount = ($votes | Where-Object { $_ -eq "DT" }).Count
if ($ltCount -ge $dtCount) { return "LT" } else { return "DT" } }
$deviceType = Get-DeviceType
if (-not $deviceType) { Write-Host -ForegroundColor Yellow "WARNING: Could not automatically determine device type. Defaulting to DT (Desktop)." $deviceType = "DT" }
Write-Host -ForegroundColor Green "Detected device type: $deviceType" #endregion
#region --- Build and Apply New Computer Name --- $newName = "$CompanyID-$deviceType-$serialSuffix"
if ($newName.Length -gt 15) { Write-Host -ForegroundColor Yellow "Warning: '$newName' exceeds 15 characters and may not be NetBIOS-compatible." }
Write-Host "Current computer name: $env:COMPUTERNAME" Write-Host "New computer name: $newName"
try { Rename-Computer -NewName $newName -Force -ErrorAction Stop Write-Host -ForegroundColor Green "Computer renamed to '$newName' successfully." Write-Host -ForegroundColor Cyan "A restart is required for the change to take effect (not performed by this script)." } catch { throw "Failed to rename computer: $($_.Exception.Message)" } #endregion Show more
Architected GUI message box solution with hidden console window Architected GUI message box solution with hidden console window Here's the updated script — it hides the PowerShell console window immediately on launch, does all the work silently in the background, then shows a message box with the results. The window won't close until you click OK.
Ran 2 commands, created a file, read a file Ran 2 commands, created a file, read a file Two changes did the trick:
Console hidden immediately — a small GetConsoleWindow/ShowWindow P/Invoke call at the top hides the PowerShell window as soon as the script starts, so the user never sees the console flash. Result shown as a message box, not console output — every Write-Host status message is now rolled into a single System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox. The script calls exit right after .Show() returns, so the process (and the hidden console) only terminates once the user clicks OK. A couple of notes on behavior:
Errors (missing serial, rename failure) now pop their own message box with an error icon and title "Rename Failed," instead of throwing to a console no one sees. Success shows old name, new name, device type, and any warnings (oversized NetBIOS name, defaulted device type) all in one dialog. Since the console is hidden but not closed until exit, if you're kicking this off from RMM as a scheduled task/deployment step, make sure whatever launches it doesn't itself wait synchronously in a way that blocks on a hidden window — you may want -WindowStyle Hidden on the invocation too, though it's technically redundant now.
Rename-Computer-Silent.ps1
Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses. <# .SYNOPSIS Detects Desktop vs Laptop via WMI/CIM, then renames the computer: MGE-DeviceType-Last7OfSerial (e.g., MGE-LT-1234567) Runs unattended in the background — console window is hidden — and reports the result in a message box. The script (and window) will not exit until the user clicks OK.
.NOTES Run as Administrator. Requires a restart (handled separately) to apply the new computer name. Designed for RMM/ZTI deployment (NinjaOne, N-central, OSDCloud, etc.) #>
#region --- Hide the console window --- Add-Type -Name Window -Namespace Console -MemberDefinition ' [DllImport("kernel32.dll")] public static extern IntPtr GetConsoleWindow(); [DllImport("user32.dll")] public static extern bool ShowWindow(IntPtr hWnd, int nCmdShow); ' # 0 = SW_HIDE [Console.Window]::ShowWindow([Console.Window]::GetConsoleWindow(), 0) | Out-Null
Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Drawing #endregion
#region --- Config --- $CompanyID = "KIME" #endregion
#region --- Helper: show message box and exit --- function Show-ResultAndExit { param( [string]$Message, [string]$Title, [System.Windows.Forms.MessageBoxIcon]$Icon ) [System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox]::Show( $Message, $Title, [System.Windows.Forms.MessageBoxButtons]::OK, $Icon ) | Out-Null exit } #endregion
#region --- Get Serial Number FIRST (bail out early if missing) --- try { $serial = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_BIOS -ErrorAction Stop).SerialNumber } catch { Show-ResultAndExit -Title "Rename Failed" -Icon Error -Message "No serial number found. Unable to query Win32_BIOS:`n$($_.Exception.Message)" }
if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($serial) -or $serial -match '^(To Be Filled By O.E.M.|None|System Serial Number|0+)$') { Show-ResultAndExit -Title "Rename Failed" -Icon Error -Message "No serial number found. Computer name was NOT changed." }
$serialSuffix = if ($serial.Length -lt 7) { $serial } else { $serial.Substring($serial.Length - 7) } #endregion
#region --- Detect Desktop vs Laptop --- function Get-DeviceType {
$pcSystemType = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_ComputerSystem -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).PCSystemType $chassisTypes = (Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_SystemEnclosure -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).ChassisTypes $hasBattery = [bool](Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Battery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
$laptopChassis = @(8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 18, 21, 30, 31, 32)
$votes = @()
if ($pcSystemType -eq 2) { $votes += "LT" } elseif ($pcSystemType -eq 1) { $votes += "DT" }
if ($chassisTypes) { $match = $chassisTypes | Where-Object { $laptopChassis -contains $_ } if ($match) { $votes += "LT" } else { $votes += "DT" } }
if ($hasBattery) { $votes += "LT" }
if ($votes.Count -eq 0) { return $null }
$ltCount = ($votes | Where-Object { $_ -eq "LT" }).Count $dtCount = ($votes | Where-Object { $_ -eq "DT" }).Count
if ($ltCount -ge $dtCount) { return "LT" } else { return "DT" } }
$deviceType = Get-DeviceType $deviceTypeWasDefaulted = $false
if (-not $deviceType) { $deviceType = "DT" $deviceTypeWasDefaulted = $true } #endregion
#region --- Build and Apply New Computer Name --- $newName = "$CompanyID-$deviceType-$serialSuffix"
$warnings = @() if ($newName.Length -gt 15) { $warnings += "'$newName' exceeds 15 characters and may not be NetBIOS-compatible." } if ($deviceTypeWasDefaulted) { $warnings += "Could not automatically determine device type — defaulted to DT (Desktop)." }
$oldName = $env:COMPUTERNAME
try { Rename-Computer -NewName $newName -Force -ErrorAction Stop } catch { Show-ResultAndExit -Title "Rename Failed" -Icon Error -Message "Failed to rename computer:`n$($_.Exception.Message)" }
$resultMessage = "Deployment Completed and Computer renamed successfully.`n`n" + "Previous name: $oldName`n" + "New name: $newName`n" + "Device type: $deviceType`n`n" + "A restart is required for the change to take effect."
if ($warnings.Count -gt 0) { $resultMessage += "`n`nWarnings:`n- " + ($warnings -join "`n- ") }
Show-ResultAndExit -Title "Rename Complete" -Icon Information -Message $resultMessage #endregion
